clinical_indications: Large kidney stones to treat with lithotripsy

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP reports

claim.label: Was the USG / X-RAY KUB along with Patient ID and Date shows stone removed?

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes, detailing signs, symptoms, examination findings, planned line of treatment &advise for admission submitted?
Was Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP reports submitted?
Was Clinical notes and Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP are indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was Post procedure USG / X-RAY KUB along with Patient ID and date report shows stone removed?
Was the Detailed Procedure/Operative notes submitted?
Is the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and imaging reports (IVP/NCCT/CT-IVP) are not indicative of the procedure.
Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking details on history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings, or advice for admission.
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP reports missing or unclear.
Detailed indoor case papers missing or incomplete, lacking daily vitals and treatment details.
Post-procedure USG / X-RAY KUB report missing, not showing stone removal, or lacking Patient ID and date.

clinical_key_pointers: Retrograde intrarenal surgery (RIRS) is described as a safe and effective technique, and a better alternative to extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy (ESWL) and percutaneous nephrolithotomy (PNL).
With advances in endourologic technology, RIRS has become second line therapy in the treatment of ESWL-resistant lower pole stones and for patients with co-morbidities.

Medical Expulsive Therapy (MET) should be offered for ureteric stones <10mm, in the absence of infection, obstruction or deranged renal function, and can be tried for up to 4 weeks (using alpha blockers like Tamsulosin, Alfuzocin, Doxazocin, Silodocin).
